description: Reviewed executable service-control actions. Canonical registry data stores only stable control identities and wrapper references; machine-local runtime details stay outside this file.

entries.inputContract: No user-supplied runtime arguments. Action runs the reviewed DevGov doctor command against the current repo and summarizes the result.
entries.rollbackNotes: Doctor is read-only. If it fails, inspect the reported registry or dashboard health errors before changing runtime state.
entries.notes: Reviewed local-only doctor action for the unified DevGov dashboard and its public aliases.

entries.inputContract: No user-supplied runtime arguments. Action checks the loopback-only service-control /health endpoint and validates canonical registries.

entries.notes: Reviewed local-only doctor action for the DevGov service-control listener. Restart is intentionally not approved because the listener is the executor.

entries.inputContract: No user-supplied runtime arguments. Action performs bounded local ensure-running behavior for the DevGov dashboard origin only.
entries.rollbackNotes: If the action fails, inspect reports/service-control-events.json and rerun the reviewed local dashboard start path manually.
entries.notes: This first approved action is intentionally bounded to local DevGov dashboard ensure-running semantics on the non-public control listener.

restartPolicy.permissionBoundary: Use reviewed local wrapper only; execution is loopback-only and must be approved as a one-click operation by the dashboard operator.

restartPolicy.rollbackExpectation: If the action fails, inspect reports/service-control-events.json and rerun the reviewed local dashboard start path manually. entries.inputContract: No user input. Read local Chrome channel model-cache state and summarize whether the Stable primary store and secondary channel links are healthy.
entries.rollbackNotes: Doctor is read-only. If it fails, inspect the reported channel drift and run Reset only after Chrome is closed.

entries.inputContract: No user input. Repair installed secondary Chrome channel model-cache links so they point to the Stable primary model store; skip non-installed channels.
entries.rollbackNotes: If reset does not restore readiness, close Chrome, inspect the channel-local .del backup, and restore the previous model directory before retrying.
entries.notes: Reviewed local-only reset action for Chrome built-in AI model cache sharing.

restartPolicy.permissionBoundary: Use reviewed local wrapper only; execution is limited to current-user Chrome channel model-cache paths and skips non-installed Chrome channels.
restartPolicy.backupExpectation: Before replacing a real secondary-channel model directory or wrong link, the wrapper moves it into the channel-local .del folder with a timestamped name. It does not permanently delete model bytes.
restartPolicy.rollbackExpectation: To roll back, close Chrome and move the timestamped .del backup back to OptGuideOnDeviceModel for the affected channel, or rerun Reset after confirming the Stable primary model store is healthy. If reset still fails, stop at manual diagnostics and do not retry blindly.
